channel.title: Notes from the Birth Centre Campaign
channel.description: Updates from the Community Birth Centre Working Group for Galway and the west of Ireland, written by Joan Hampton.

item.title: Why continuity of carer sits at the heart of the proposal
item.author: joan.hampton@Finthesiss.ai (Joan Hampton)
item.description: A familiar midwife through pregnancy, birth, and the postnatal weeks is not a luxury. The evidence links it to fewer preterm births and far higher satisfaction. This is the spine of the case we are taking to consultation.

item.title: What the feasibility study actually found
item.author: joan.hampton@Finthesiss.ai (Joan Hampton)
item.description: Between 28 and 35 percent of bookings at the hospital could safely be offered a birth centre. Three candidate sites, all within 14 minutes of the unit, have now been assessed. A centre serving 350 to 450 families a year is viable.

item.title: Bring your questions to the public consultation
item.author: joan.hampton@Finthesiss.ai (Joan Hampton)
item.description: The public consultation opens at Galway City Hall on the morning of 10 October 2026. Whether you support the idea or have concerns about transfer times and rural access, we want to hear from you.

item.title: Thank you to everyone who came to City Hall
item.author: joan.hampton@Finthesiss.ai (Joan Hampton)
item.description: The room was full and the survey responses are still coming in. Continuity of carer and a calmer setting came up again and again. We are compiling the themes now and will share a summary before the strategy day in January.
